---
title: "Session06"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Session06 Priors (Belief)

The student-t distribution

-   bit squashed distribution

-   bit thicker tails : higher distribution on the lower possible values - liberal

-   good when we dont have alot of data

-   Normal (df is 1) - Cauchy (df is infinite)

## Priors in linear models

![](images/clipboard-3154732203.png)

![](images/clipboard-1274535851.png)

## How to specify priors - Think!!

-   does the parameter have **natural bounds**

-   what **distribution** does the parameter have

-   what are plausible **values ranges**? - domain knowledge

![](images/clipboard-2223513033.png)

## Weakly informative priors

-   assigning the highest probability to an effect of zero

-   still constrain the plausible values

![](images/clipboard-1543461647.png)
